diagrams.title: Greedy Algorithm Decision Process
diagrams.description: A flowchart showing the step-by-step decision process of a greedy algorithm: define the problem, identify the greedy choice property, make the locally optimal choice at each step, and verify that choices lead to global optimal solution through optimal substructure.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the general framework and decision-making process of greedy algorithms, helping students understand when and how to apply greedy approach

diagrams.title: Activity Selection Problem Visualization
diagrams.description: A horizontal timeline showing multiple activities with start and end times. Demonstrates how the greedy algorithm selects the maximum number of non-overlapping activities by always choosing the activity that finishes earliest and doesn't conflict with previously selected activities.
elements: Activity bars with different colors (selected vs not selected)
Start/end time labels on each activity
Counter showing number of selected activities
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the classic activity selection problem and demonstrates the greedy choice of picking the earliest finishing activity to maximize the number of non-conflicting activities

diagrams.title: Greedy vs Dynamic Programming Comparison
diagrams.description: A side-by-side comparison showing a problem instance where greedy fails. Uses a simple knapsack example with items having weights and values, showing how fractional knapsack (greedy works) differs from 0/1 knapsack (greedy may fail). Includes a visual representation of the optimal solution obtained by each method.
elements: Visual indicator of which items are selected
Note box explaining why greedy works for fractional but not 0/1 knapsack
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the critical difference between problems where greedy works (greedy choice property) versus those requiring DP, helping students understand when to apply greedy algorithms
